DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM ROBBINS'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET #4) IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE GENERAL OBJECTIONS ARE STRICKEN AS THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE DOES NOT PROVIDE FOR SUCH OBJECTIONS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS 37 AND 38. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND STRICKEN. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO 39 AND 40 AS THE REQUESTS ARE OVERBROAD AND SEEK INFORMATION THAT IS IRRELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE ACTION. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMPLIANCE DATE IS AUGUST 4, 2006. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents From Deft Intercontinental Hotels Group
Off Calendar Jul-28-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
JUDITH A. GRAZIANO, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, WILLIAM ROBBINS
JEFFREY M. VUCINICH, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY

MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. Plaintiff William Robbins's motion to compel further responses and production of documents (set #4) is granted in part and denied in part. The general objections are stricken as the Code of Civil Procedure does not provide for such objections. The motion is granted as to requests 37 and 38. The objections are overruled and stricken. The motion is denied as to 39 and 40 as the requests are overbroad and seek information that is irrelevant to the subject matter of the action. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMPLIANCE DATE IS AUGUST 4, 2006. = (610/BEC) COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Schindler Elevator Corp.'S Compliance With Subpoena And For Sanctions, Including Contempt And Forfeiture, And For An Award Of Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs;
Off Calendar Jan-09-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nandor Krause for defendant and opposing party: Schindler Elevator Corporation

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFCATION.
Defendants' motion to compel compliance with subpoenas is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to Phil Strickland. Counsel for Schindler Elevator is ordered to provide the last known address for the witness to enable Defendant to serve the former employee with a subpoena. The motion is granted as to Elvis Saunders as counsel for Schindler previously agreed to accept service. The motion is granted as to Schindler Elevator. The objections are overruled. If any documents are to be withheld based on the attorney client or attorney work product privilege, they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log. Counsel are ordered to meet and confer as to scheduling the deposition of the Corporation and Elvis Saunders at a mutually convenient time. Said depositions are to be completed on or before February 6, 2006. Responsive documents are to be produced at least two business days before the dates scheduled for deposition. Pursuant to CCP Section 1987.2, the Court orders that monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,116.30 be paid on or before February 6, 2006.
MODIFICATION: That award of sanctions is stayed. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
